subject: Chemistry
solution:
Statement A is incorrect as the complex is yellow-green, not red.
Statement B is incorrect because the complex shows low solubility in water, especially at neutral pH.
Statement C is incorrect because Ni(II) in square planar geometry has all d-electrons paired (d^8 configuration), not unpaired electrons.
Statement D is correct regarding the N-Ni-N bond angle being approximately 90° in square planar geometry.
Statement E is correct as the complex contains two five-membered metallacycles (chelate rings) per dimethylglyoxime molecule.
The incorrect statements are B, C, and E.

subject: Chemistry
solution:
The formula Mg(NH4)PO4 represents magnesium ammonium phosphate it is white in colour. K3CoNO26 is known as potassium hexanitritocobaltate(III). It is yellow in colour. The yellow colour is due to the presence of the cobalt ion (Co3+) in the complex, which absorbs certain wavelengths of light and reflects yellow light.  MnOOH2is brown in colour. Fe4FeCN63 is blue in colour. 

Complex Colour
MgNH4PO4 White 
K3CoNO26 Yellow
MnOOH2 Brown
Fe4FeCN63 Blue

subject: Chemistry
solution:
Homoleptic complexes are compounds in which all the ligands bound to the central metal atom or ion are identical.

Homoleptic complexes have only one type of ligand.

Ni(CN)42- has only one type of ligand.

Hence, option A is the answer.

subject: Chemistry
solution:
Complex
(i) FeF63-Fe3+⇒[Ar]3 d5, F- is WFL

n=5,  μ=35 B.M

(ii)CoNH363+⇒Co3+Ar3 d6,NH3 is SFL

n=0, μ=0 B.M

(iii) NiCl42-Ni2+⇒Ar3 d8,Cl-→WFL

n=2, μ=8 B.M

(iv) CuNH342+Cu2+⇒Ar3 d9,NH3→SFL

n=1, μ=3 B.M

Thus correct order of spin only magnetic moment is

(i)>(iii)>(iv)>(ii)

subject: Chemistry
solution:
A spin-only magnetic moment occurs when an electron rotates on its own axis and generates a magnetic field.

μ = n ( n + 2 )BM
Here, n is the number of unpaired electrons.
The unit for a spin-only magnetic moment is the Bohr magneton (BM).

MnH2O62+

Mn2+=3d5

μ=5(5+2)=5.91BM

subject: Chemistry
solution:
d-orbital is degenerate which split into two levels eg and t2g in the presence of ligands. This splitting is due to the presence of ligands. This is called the crystal-field splitting and the energy difference between the two levels is called the crystal-field splitting energy, Δ0.

(A)  Ti2+:4s03d2

t2g e–=2  eg e–=0 

Δ0=2-.4Δ0=-.8Δ0

(B)  V2+:4s03d3

t2g e–=3  eg e–=0 

Δ0=3-.4Δ0

= -1.2Δ0

(C)  Mn3+=4s03d4

t2g e–=3eg e–=1 

Δ0=3-.4Δ0+1.6Δ0

=-1.2Δ0+0.6Δ0

= -0.6Δ0

(D)  Fe3+=4 s03d5

t2g e–=3  eg e–=2 

Δ0=3-.4Δ0+2.6Δ0

=-1.2Δ0+1.2Δ0=0

Degenerate orbitals of CrH2O63+: 

Orbitals having same energy call degenerate orbital, And here water acts as weak ligands which split d-orbital into two parts.


Hence, according to the options given, degenerate orbitals are dxz and dyz.

subject: Chemistry
solution:
Cobalt chloride when dissolved in water forms pink coloured isCoH2O6Cl2 . This is due to the electronic transition of an electron from t2g to eg energy level of CoH2O62+ complex. When excess of HCl is added to the solution CoH2O62+ is transformed into deep blue colour complex [CoCl4]2−.

△t= 49 △o

△t < △o

Tetrahedral complexes have smaller crystal field splitting than octahedral complexes.

CoCl2+6H2O⟶CoH2O6Cl2 

CoH2O62+ →Conc. HCl [CoCl4]2-

Hence, X=CoH2O62+, Y=CoCl42-, Z=Tetrahedral

subject: Chemistry
solution:
In facial isomer, three Cl atoms present on one face of the octahedron. Similarly, three ammonia molecules are present on one face of the octahedron. All 3 same ligands have 90° bond angle between them.

The Cl-Co-Cl bond angle in above octahedral complex is 90°

options: Both Statement I and Statement II are incorrect

solution:
In NiH2O62+, H2O is a weak field ligand. Therefore, there are unpaired electrons in Ni2+. In this complex, the d electrons from the lower energy level can be excited to the higher energy level i.e., the possibility of d-dtransition is present. Hence, NiH2O62+ is coloured.

In NiCN42-, the electrons are all paired as CN- is a strong field ligand. Therefore, d-d transition is not possible in NiCN42-. Hence, it is colourless.

NiH2O6+2→Green colour solution due to d-d transition.

Ni(CN)4-2→is diamagnetic and it is colourless.

options: A is false but R is true

solution:
[Fe(H2O)6]3+  has magnetic moment value of 5.92 BM because H2O  is a weak ligand and pairing will not occur. It involves sp3d2 hybridisation with five unpaired electrons (because magnetic moment is equal to 5.92 BM) and outer orbital complex is formed. Magnetic moment can be calculated by the formula μm=n(n+2) BM where, μm=magnetic moment, n=number of unpaired electron(s).

μm=1.74 BM, n=1μm=5.92 BM, n=5 

In [Fe(CN)6]3– complex CN− is stronger ligand than H2O according to spectrochemical series.  Hence, four electrons will pair itself. It involves d2sp3 hybridisation with one unpaired electron and inner orbital complex is formed and has a value of only 1.74 BM.

In Bothe the complexes the oxidation state of iron is +3.

Dianionic complex with coordination number four
solution:
Lead chromate is yellow in color due to their absorption and reflection of specific wavelengths of light.

PbCrO4 + NaOH (hot excess) →[Pb(OH)4] -2 + Na2CrO4

The complex formed is PbOH42-

which has coordination number =4 and complex is dianionic due to presence of -2 charge.

coordination number, the number of atoms, ions, or molecules that a central atom or ion holds as its nearest neighbours in a complex or coordination compound or in a crystal.

subject: Chemistry
solution:
Crystal field splitting depends upon the nature of ligand. The nature of ligand $\Delta$ decreases as shown below
$\mathrm{C}_2 \mathrm{O}_4^{--} < \mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O} < \mathrm{NH}_3 < \mathrm{CN}^{-}$
hence the crystal field splitting will be maximum for $\left[\mathrm{Co}(\mathrm{CN})_6\right]^{3-}$

solution:
(A) $\left[\mathrm{Fe}(\mathrm{CN})_5 \mathrm{NO}\right]^{-2} \rightarrow$ Heteroleptic, $\mathrm{Fe}^{+2}, 3 \mathrm{~d}^6$, $\mathrm{t}_{2 \mathrm{~g}}{ }^6 \mathrm{e}_{\mathrm{g}}{ }^0, \mathrm{~d}^2 \mathrm{sp}^3$, Low spin ( 3 d series +SFL )
(B) $\left[\mathrm{CoF}_6\right]^{-3} \rightarrow$ Homoleptic, $\mathrm{sp}^3 \mathrm{~d}^2$, High spin, $\mathrm{Co}^{+3}, 3 \mathrm{~d}^6(3 \mathrm{~d}$ series + WFL $)$
(C) $\left[\mathrm{Fe}(\mathrm{CN})_6\right]^{-4} \rightarrow$ Homoleptic
$\mathrm{Fe}^{+2}, 3 \mathrm{~d}^6, \mathrm{~d}^2 \mathrm{sp}^3, \mathrm{t}_{2 \mathrm{~g}}{ }^6 \mathrm{eg}^0$ Low spin
$(3 \mathrm{~d}$ series +SFL$)$
(D) $\left[\mathrm{Co}\left(\mathrm{NH}_3\right)_6\right]^{+3} \rightarrow$ Homoleptic, $\mathrm{Co}^{+3} 3 \mathrm{~d}^6, \mathrm{~d}^2 \mathrm{sp}^3$,
$\mathrm{t}_{2 \mathrm{~g}}{ }^6 \mathrm{eg}^0$, Low spin (3d series + SFL)
(E) $\left[\mathrm{Cr}\left(\mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O}\right)_6\right]^{+2} \rightarrow$ Homoleptic
$\mathrm{Cr}^{+2} 3 \mathrm{~d}^4, \mathrm{~d}^2 \mathrm{sp}^3$, High spin $\mathrm{t}_{2 \mathrm{~g}}{ }^3 \mathrm{e}_{\mathrm{g}}{ }^1$
(3d series + WFL)

subject: Chemistry
solution:
In Fe(CO)4C2O4+

Oxidation no. of Fe=+3 

26Fe+3=Ar183 d54 s0 

CO is a Strong field ligand, so paring occurs against the Hund's rule. 

Fe+3=t2g2,2,1 eg0,0 

Number of unpaired electron =1 

so magnetic moment μ=nn+2 B.M. =11+2=3=1.73 BM
